title: Warmup separates transient from steady state
description: Early iterations pay JIT compilation, cold caches, and lazy initialization costs that do not reflect steady-state performance.
body: Use when interpreting why early benchmark iterations are slower. The first runs absorb JIT/AOT compilation, cold CPU and disk caches, lazy class/module loading, and allocator warmup, so including them conflates transient and steady-state cost. Do not average cold and warm iterations together when steady-state throughput is the question. Actions: discard or separately report warmup iterations, then measure steady state. Validation signals include a per-iteration time curve that flattens after warmup. Risk boundary: when cold start is the actual user-facing metric, measuring only steady state hides the real cost; choose warm vs cold deliberately based on what users experience.
